title: web -- Fetch/Search/Open Cheatsheet (URL-as-target banned, grap required for tab-bound verbs)
desc: wkappbot web tools. Exempt URL-takers: open (new tab), fetch (raw HTTP), search (query), tabs/status/close (CDP-level). Tab-bound subcommands (navigate, html, url, title, capture, run, file) REQUIRE a grap as first positional and reject bare URLs at the dispatcher. Every command that touches a specific tab prints OK <grap> on stdout as reconnection confirmation. Title field in grap is permitted only when the match itself came via title, and only the pattern literal (not the full window title), capped at 30 chars.
steps: Exempt (URL/query OK): open <url> launches a new tab; fetch <url> raw HTTP GET (no browser); search <query> Google via CDP; tabs, status, close are CDP-port level

Tab-bound (GRAP REQUIRED): navigate, html, url, title, capture, run, file. First positional MUST be a grap; a URL (http/https/file/chrome/about/javascript/data scheme) triggers a uniform [WEB:<VERB>] grap-required error pointing to a11y find + web open

Standard error text: grap required as first positional. URL-as-target is banned. Discover grap: wkappbot a11y find "*Chrome*" --deep. Example: wkappbot web <verb> "{proc:'chrome',hwnd:0x...}" [args]. Open new tab via web open <url>
Reconnection contract: every web command that operates on a tab emits OK <grap> on STDOUT (in addition to [WEB] TARGET on stderr). Callers can parse stdout for the exact grap to feed the next command

Title rule: title field is emitted in grap only when the find match came via title (MatchedVia==title). Even then, only the pattern literal core is used (stripped of wildcards, capped 30 chars). Rationale: titles mutate (tab switches, dirty-doc *), so full-title capture invites miss-match on the next copy-paste call
Example chain: wkappbot web open https://example.com -> parse OK {proc:'chrome',hwnd:0x..,domain:'example.com'} -> wkappbot a11y read "{proc:'chrome',hwnd:0x..}" -> reliable target lock
web read is REMOVED outright. Use a11y read with a grap instead
fetch is for raw HTTP where no JS is needed (static HTML, API endpoints); JS-rendered content belongs to a11y read on an open tab
author: claude
